    Analysis 
    
            
    
                
    
                
    
            
    
        
    
    
    

    
    
        
    
            
    Student Help 
    
            
    
                
    1. Quote Select
    
                
    Choose quotes from the source (tap and drag where your teacher allows) and work through the stages your lesson uses. Send quotes to the Writing Desk when you are ready.
    
                
    I'm stuck — get help (bar under the question): open it to turn options on or off:
    
                
      
                    
    • Quote choices from the bank (needs at least two quotes in the lesson). This shows the card-style rounds; highlighting in the passage still works when Tap is on for the level.
      • 
                    
    • Synonym suggestions on the Writing Desk.
      • 
                    
    • In the standalone student app only: Show sentence-bank chips on Writing Desk — draggable markers, starters, and analysis phrases. Until you tick this, the sentence bank shows a short reminder instead of chips.
      • 
                    
    • Optional glosses, language/tone/structure labels, and short sentence hints from the lesson (not full model answers).
      • 
                
    
                
    
                
    2. Writing Desk
    
                
    Write or build your answer here. Drag chips into the box or click a chip to insert at the cursor. Your teacher may use sentence structure and saved quotes from Quote Select.
    
                
    When sentence-bank chips are available, they are grouped into collapsible sections (e.g. Markers, Starters, Methods, Analysis / points, Links) so you can open only what you need.
    
                
    If a quote in the bank uses … in the middle, the app checks it against the passage in chunks — a red "Not found in passage" note usually means spelling differs or the gap doesn't match the real text.

    Teacher Settings  
    
            
    
                
    
                    
                    
                    
                    
                
    
                
    
                
    
                    🎁 Reward (Level Complete)
                    
    After a level is completed, students may see a Play Reward button that opens your link (optional time limit and frequency below).
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                        
    Leave empty to hide the reward button.
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                        
    0 = no limit. When set, the button can lock until the time has passed or the student completes another level.
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                        
    0 = offer after every qualifying completion; otherwise only when the level number is a multiple of N.
    
                    
    
                
    
                
    

                
    
                
    
                    📄 Component 1 — source passage
                    
    This is the same source text and rich-text toolbar as Load & AI → Source Text (one #ai-input in the page — it moves here when you use the Editing Suite so you can edit passage and banks in one place). For Component 2 lessons this block is hidden; use Component 2 — passages below.
    
                    
    
                
    
                
    
                    📑 Component 2 — passages (21st / 19th)
                    
    Use this when the lesson has two extracts (21st-century resource + 19th-century question-paper passage). Switch Editing with the buttons, then paste or tidy each text. Which passage(s) apply per level is set in Level Bank → C2 texts (Both lets students use the 21st C / 19th C toggle in the header or Quote Select). The Passage column on Load & AI is stored on each question for export/reference; it does not override the in-game passage.
    
                    
    Dual 21st / 19th passage editors apply only in Component 2. This lesson is Component 1 — edit the passage in Component 1 — source passage above (or Load & AI; it is the same editor). To use two passages: set Past papers → Load as to Component 2 and re-import, or paste a pooled source using the —— Text —— delimiter.
     (copies your current source into the 21st C passage; add 19th C in the editor or re-import)
    
                    
    
                        
    
                            
    
                                
                                Editing:
                                
                                
                            
    
                            
    
                                
                                
                                
                                
                                
                                
                                
                            
    
                            
    
                            
    Same as the student 21st C / 19th C source toggle (header or Quote Select). Changes save into the lesson layout.
    
                        
    
                    
    
                
    
                
    
                    🎚️ Level Bank (1–10)
                    
    Controls language tier, complexity (meanings/analysis), and which question applies to each level. Students use tap text (select from passage) by default.
    
                    
    Question: one per level — use dropdown to assign or change; choose — None — to clear.
    
                    
    You can list each paper part as its own row in Questions (e.g. Q1a, Q1b). The Level Bank decides what students see: assign one question per level; Quote Select shows that row only. With one id per level, when the points goal is met the app moves to the next active level (next bank assignment). Use multiple ids on one level only if you need several questions on the same level before advancing.
    
                    
    Worksheet (Word): use Focus questions — worksheet (WS) below to tick WS on each exam question you want in the export (same control as Load & AI).
    
                    
    Component 2: the C2 texts column (after Tap) sets whether each level shows the 21st-century passage, the 19th-century passage, or both. It appears for Component 2 lessons and when two passages are available; scroll horizontally if the table is wider than the window.
    
                    
    LoR (levels of response): long-mark style (e.g. 10 marks). Set Points to the question total. Quote/evidence tallies and collapsible point/evidence indicators on the Writing Desk; mark-scheme advice is collapsible there too. Students can switch Quote Select ↔ Writing Desk anytime.
    
                    
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
    Same rows as Load & AI → Focus questions. Tick WS to include that question on the Word worksheet (with quote bank Include on worksheet). Off by default for every row.
    
                        
    
                        
                    
    
                
    

                
    
                    ✍️ Analysis Banks Editor
                    
    
                        Lesson JSON and each quote’s modelAnswer slots add short scaffold phrases here (duplicates skipped). Prefer hints students combine, not copy-paste paragraphs.
                        
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                        
                        
                    
    
                    
    
                        Link phrases (optional — short connectors; also appear as desk chips for that level)
                        
    
                            
                            
                        
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
    
                            
                            
                        
    
                        
    
                    
    
                
    

                
    
                    Quote Selection
                    
    
                        
                    
    
                    
    Quotes are set by you (quote bank below) or by students tapping the passage. For I'm stuck, add ≥2 quotes for multiple-choice mode, or let students enable short meanings / device focus / rubric hints (truncated from the same JSON fields — not full answers). Include on worksheet must be ticked on each quote you want in the Word export (defaults off; use bulk buttons under the bank).
    
                    
    
                        
    Teacher: tap text & record answers
    
                        
                        
                        
                    
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                    
    
                    
    
                        Quote bank (optional)
                        
    Add or edit quotes here. If you add at least 2, students can use "I'm stuck — give me quote choices to pick from" to see multiple-choice rounds as well as tapping the passage when Tap is on for that level. Wrong quotes are only alternate quotations for the quote multiple-choice grid (one line per quote — no justifications). Use + Add bad quote for weak lines, or Copy AI prompt for wrong quotes to draft near-miss lines from the passage. For plausible wrong explanations about the same quote (worksheet chips / justify stage), use Distractor points and Copy AI prompt for distractor points.
    
                        
    Hybrid workflow (shorter AI): (1) Import past paper and run Extract text (includes mark scheme PDF) so indicative bullets are in the lesson. (2) Suggest quotes from mark scheme — local match of bullets to the passage (no AI). (3) AI tab → Compact enrich prompt — small prompt with questions + bullets + your quotes only; merge JSON to fill meanings, modelAnswer, and wrong-quote fields (distractors in JSON). Full unified prompt is optional if you need the model to invent every quote.
    
                        
    
                            
                            Needs extracted mark scheme + source text. Skips quotes already in the bank.
                        
    
                        
    
                            Show:
                            
                            
                            
                            |
                            Worksheet:
                            
                            
                        
    
                        
    
                        
    
                            
                            
                        
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
    Clears chosen quotes, desk queue, and related session state for Quote Select / desk.
    
                    
    
                
    

                
    
                    ⚙️ Sentence Structure
                    
    
                        
                        
                        
                        
                    
    
                    
    Use the buttons to duplicate the six builder slots and Tone/Justify tab settings; Level Bank question assignments are unchanged. Link is a single slot per level (uses that level’s link bank — see Analysis Banks).
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
    Chips come from the Analysis Banks (Markers, Starters, Methods, Analysis) plus this level’s Link bank. Load JSON and Merge from quotes fill those lists; the same phrases support sentence building, worksheet rubrics, and I'm stuck hints — no separate chip list required.
    
                        
    
                            
                        
    
                        
    
                            Optional: extra chip lines (legacy)
                            
    One phrase per line, appended after bank phrases (advanced).
    
                            
                        
    
                    
    
                
    

                
    
                    🏗️ Global Structure Questions
                    
    
                    
                
    
                
    

                
    
                
                
    
                    Teacher Guide (Click to collapse)
                    
    
                        
    🤖 AI Lesson Generator (Important)
    
                        
      
                            
    1. Paste your Source Text and Focus Question below.
      2. 
                            
    2. Click Generate Prompt.
      3. 
                            
    3. Copy the generated prompt text.
      4. 
                            
    4. Paste it into ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, or Gemini.
      5. 
                            
    5. Copy the JSON code the AI gives you. (If the AI adds text before/after the JSON, copy only the part from { to }.)
      6. 
                            
    6. Paste that JSON into the box below and click Load Data. Wrapped output {"lesson":{ ... }} is fine — the app unwraps it. Keep your full source passage in the Source Text box when loading so the lesson is not replaced by an AI synopsis.
      7. 
                        
    
                        
    ⚙️ Structure & Banks
    
                        
    Customize the sentence builders for Levels 1-3. Select 'point' to use AI-generated analysis points.
    
                        
    📄 Worksheet
    
                        
    Exports a compact Word doc (exercises only — no full passage): (1) match quote → meaning from AI translation, (2) rubric / sentence scaffolds from modelAnswer slots + banks, (3) match quote → Language / Tone / Structure from AI type. Optional teacher key. Under I'm stuck — get help, students only see short hints (same underlying fields, truncated in the app).
    
                    
    
                
    

                
    
                    
    🤖 AI Lesson Generator
    
                    
    
                        
                        
    Export includes only rows where WS is ticked here (plus quote bank Include). Meanings and points export as jumbled match tasks. Language/Tone/Structure match appears only if two or more of those devices are on under Editing suite → Selection devices; quotes whose type is switched off are omitted.

                        Component 2 (two passages): the rich-text editor (bold, italic, …) is not on this tab — switch to Editing Suite at the top, then use Component 2 — passages (21st / 19th buttons). Past-paper text fills those boxes from the Resource PDF (21st) and Questions PDF page for 19th C; run Extract text (or the 19th / resource re-extract buttons) if a box is empty.
                        
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
    Component 1: same source box as Editing Suite → Component 1 — source passage (moves with the tab). Component 2: passages under Editing Suite → Component 2.
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
    
                        
    
                    
    
                    
    Lesson item count for AI generation. Students select quotes by tapping the source text.
    
                    
    
                        
                        
    
                            
                            
                            
                        
    
                        
    Controls both AI prompts and student app exports.
    
                    
    
                    
                    
                
    
                    
                    
    
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                    
    
                    
                    
                    
    
                        Compact AI — enrich existing quotes only
                        
    Does not include the full reading passage — only your focus questions, a few mark-scheme bullets, and up to 16 good quotes (truncated). Use after Suggest quotes from mark scheme or hand-picked quotes. Paste the model’s JSON array back and merge — then run Merge from quotes again in Analysis Banks if needed.
    
                        
    
                            
                            
                        
    
                        
                        
                        
                        
                    
    
                
    
                
    
                
    

                
    
                
    
                    📄 Import past paper
                    
    
                        ?
                        
    
                            
    Upload the PDFs for a past paper. Use Extract text then Extract questions (or paste past-paper JSON and use Load JSON in this Editing Suite panel — loading a lesson clears cached PDFs in this browser so you choose files again for this paper).
    
                            
    Extract text caches PDFs and updates the resource, questions-PDF passages (including Component 2 layout for the 21st C resource text and the 19th C extract from the question paper), and mark scheme. It does not change the question bank. Then use Extract questions to split Section A reading tasks into the bank. If Extract questions finds fewer than two items and you left the Section A reading page field empty (Component 2), the app may retry from the full Questions PDF; if you set specific pages, that widening is skipped.
    
                            
    At least one PDF is required. Component 1: resource page(s) empty → defaults 2–3; question page(s) empty → whole PDF. Component 2 when a page box is left empty: Resource defaults to pages 2–3 (type all for every page). Section A reading defaults to page 2. 19th C passage defaults to page 3. Mark scheme: 5–13 if empty. After changing files or page fields, run Extract text then Extract questions (or the per-part Re-extract … buttons).
    
                            
    PDFs are stored in IndexedDB when you choose files or run Extract text, so Extract questions and Re-extract … still work after a reload. Filenames (not file data) are saved in your lesson JSON; exports for students omit PDF cache.
    
                        
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                        
                        
    
                            
                            
                        
    
                        
    
                            
    
                            
    
                        
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                        
                        
    
                            
                            
                        
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                        
                        
    
                            
                            
                        
    
                    
    
                    
                    
                    
                    
                    
                    
                
    
                
    
                    📌 Template
                    
    
                        ?
                        
    
                            
    Save your current configuration (sentence builder, level bank, device types, etc.) under a name, or import a template file to apply its settings. Only one template is active; importing replaces current settings and shows the template name.
    
                        
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                    
    
                    
    
                
    
                
    
                    
    📦 Export
    
                    
    
                        ?
                        
    
                            
    Worksheet tasks use WS focus questions and quotes with Include on worksheet ticked (off by default — choose after meanings/points are ready). Focus question text is cleaned for Word (stray marks/numbers). Near-duplicate quotes merge on export. For Component 1 retrieval-style Q1, the optional fields under the first focus row (model short answer + distractors) add a Short answer practice block on the Word export whenever the model text is filled — you do not need WS ticked on Q1 for that block (WS still controls which focus prompts and quotes appear in the main match/rubric sections).
    
                            
    
                                What the AI JSON supplies (worksheet + I'm stuck)
                                
      
                                    
    • Per item: original (quote), translation (short gloss) + translationDistractors, type (language / tone / structure), effect + effectDistractors (brief hints), modelAnswer as short slot strings (MARKER / STARTER / METHOD / POINT / LINK…), optional per-slot fields; distractors = alternate quotations for quote MC (quotes only — use distractorPoints for wrong explanations). distractorPoints = flawed analysis lines for the same quote (worksheet / justify).
      • 
                                    
    • Lesson: sentenceConfig per level (which rubric rows appear), optional top-level banks, questions array, sourceText (not printed on the compact worksheet).
      • 
                                    
    • I'm stuck reads only lesson quote rows (good quotes in the bank for this level/question): checkbox Meanings → each row's translation; Language/tone/structure → type; Short sentence hints → parsed modelAnswer slots. It does not use the Writing Desk essay, questionEssays, or Quote Select drafts — those are stored separately in browser state.
      • 
                                    
    • Sentence bank chips come from Analysis Banks (Markers, Starters, Methods, Analysis, Link). Merge from quotes again copies phrases out of each item's modelAnswer string, optional marker/starter/method/link, and effect (into Analysis). translation is for meanings / worksheet match — it is not merged into those chips. If the model left modelAnswer empty, banks stay at defaults until you edit banks or rebuild model lines. In standalone student mode, draggable chips appear on the Writing Desk only when the student turns on I'm stuck → Show sentence-bank chips on Writing Desk; teachers always see chips while authoring.
      • 
                                
    
                            
    
                        
    
                    
    
                    
    
                        
                        
                        
                        
                    
    
                    
    
                        Match game (meanings):
                        
                    
    
                    
    
                        Rubric (sentence elements):
                        
                    
    
                    
                    
    
                        Max unique quotes on worksheet:
                        
                        Duplicates merged by text; cap limits sheet length.
                    
    
                
    

                
                
    
            
    
        
    
    
    

    
    
        
    
            
    Answer Architect — Instruction Manual 
    
            
    
                
    Quick start
    
                
      
                    
    1. Open the app (teacher mode). Use the ⚙️ Settings button to configure the lesson.
      2. 
                    
    2. Add content: paste Source Text and Focus Question (e.g. in AI tab → generate prompt → copy to ChatGPT/Claude → paste JSON back and Load Data), or Load JSON or build manually in Edit.
      3. 
                    
    3. The app opens on Quote Select when selection is available; students can switch to Writing Desk when ready.
      4. 
                    
    4. Turn on Active Modules: Quote Select, Writing Desk. Enabling Quote Select automatically enables free select (tap text) for active levels.
      5. 
                    
    5. In Level Bank, assign language tier, complexity, and one question per level. Students select quotes by tapping the source text.
      6. 
                    
    6. Export: Worksheet (Word) or Student App (standalone HTML).
      7. 
                    
    7. Past paper PDFs: Use Extract text for passages and mark scheme, then Extract questions for Section A reading. If fewer than two questions are found and you left Question page(s) open to the whole PDF, the app may retry from the full Questions PDF. If you set specific pages (e.g. Section A reading = 2 in Component 2), it stays on those pages only. Run both again after you change page numbers or replace a file.
      8. 
                
    
                
    Modules
    
                
      
                    
    • Quote Select 🎯 — Students choose Strong/Not strong, match evidence type, justify. Level Bank controls how many good-quote and bad-quote options appear and the Quote Selection Tab Modes per level.
      • 
                    
    • Writing Desk ✍️ — Students draft the final answer. Chips from Analysis Banks (markers, starters, methods, analysis/points, per-level links) appear in separate collapsible sections on the Sentence bank. Teacher mode: chips are always available while you author. Standalone student export: draggable sentence-bank chips appear only if the student opens I'm stuck — get help and ticks Show sentence-bank chips on Writing Desk (other I'm stuck options control meanings, device labels, rubric hints, quote multiple-choice, synonyms). Practice Mode locks level and turns off Auto progress where configured.
      • 
                
    
                
    Quote bank & mark scheme (teachers)
    
                
      
                    
    • Suggest quotes from mark scheme matches indicative bullets to sentences in the source using word overlap rules (not fuzzy substring tricks), filters common exam-rubric noise, and prefers bullets whose named anchors (e.g. proper names) appear in the matched sentence. If an old row has the wrong "Good reasons" text, delete it or fix it and re-paste/re-extract the mark scheme before running suggest again.
      • 
                    
    • The quote bank warns Not found in passage when normalized text doesn't match; quotes with middle ellipsis (…) are checked in ordered segments, so shortened quotes should still follow the real wording on each side of the gap.
      • 
                
    
                
    Settings summary
    
                
    Reward tab: optional external link after level complete (minutes / every N levels). Editing Suite: Level Bank, questions, quote bank, sentence structure, Writing Desk sentence lines. Practice Mode (auto-disables Auto progress), Practice Level, Worksheet Match Mode. Quote Select and Writing Desk are always active. A level is active when it has one or more questions selected in the Level Bank.
    
                
    Sharing the Student App (hosting + QR code)
    
                
    Host the exported HTML for free: e.g. Netlify Drop (app.netlify.com/drop — drag and drop the file), Neocities (neocities.org), or GitHub Pages. Use the URL where the lesson opens. Create a QR code at qr-monkey.com: choose URL, paste your lesson link, generate and download. Students scan the QR code to open the lesson on their device.
    
                
    Top tips
    
                
      
                    
    • Quote Select: students tap the passage to select quotes. Ensure source text is set and tap text is enabled for the level.
      • 
                    
    • Student App opens on Quote Select when selection is available.
      • 
                    
    • Fill Analysis Banks (or use Merge from quotes again) so the Writing Desk sentence bank has useful chips; in standalone mode, remind students to enable chips under I'm stuck if you want them to see scaffolds.
      • 
                    
    • Fewer steps for weaker students: use Beginner stages; lower quote target and weak quotes (bad-quote count) in Level Bank.
      • 
                    
    • One level only: Practice Mode (Auto progress turns off automatically), set Practice Level, and assign questions only to that level in the Level Bank.
      • 
                    
    • Level Bank defines language tier and question per level; a level is active when it has one or more questions selected.
      •
